prompt: I'm building a B2B SaaS for small accounting firms that want to automate client tax filing. The firm admin creates the account, adds accountants, accountants upload client docs, the system extracts data and pre-fills tax forms, then the accountant reviews and submits to the tax authority. There are two user types: firm admin and accountant. Map the full customer journey for the accountant persona.

assertions.text: Output contains between 4 and 8 clearly labeled phases
Every phase has a 'Happy path' section with numbered steps
Every phase has an 'Exceptions' section with at least one exception
Output contains a valid Mermaid diagram (```mermaid code block with graph TD or graph LR)
Journey focuses on the accountant persona, not the firm admin (admin setup steps are excluded or minimal)
Steps describe what the user sees/does, not system internals (no 'database writes', 'API calls', etc.)
All exception flows resolve — they either rejoin the happy path or reach a defined end state
Output starts with an Overview section describing the journey scope and primary user
